programmes.show_description: The Maltbys are back and Pauline must battle her demons. Tiger and Joey get into trouble with a biker gang. S8 E3
An old school friend of Terry's escapes prison with just three months left of his sentence; Terry wonders why and gets him involved with some heavies from over the water. S2 E10: The Old School Tie
Alf attempts to power Else's wheelchair with a lawnmower engine. It doesn't go to plan but he does manage to foil a bank robbery. S1 E6
Jo sees a mouse in her bedroom and refuses to sleep in there; much to Chrissy's dismay, Jo swaps with Robin while the mouse is on the loose. S3 E4: Of Mice And Women
The crew of the television holiday programme 'Wish You Were Here' - along with its presenter Judith Chalmers - are at the hotel. S3 E3: Close Up
A selection of live performances from TV music shows in the 1960s for a genuine taste of the best of the '60s!
'70s sitcom starring Jimmy Jewel and Hylda Baker as brother and sister Eli and Nellie Pledge, who are forced to run a family pickle factory together in Lancashire. S6, E3: The Female of the Species
Hit '70s sitcom starring Jimmy Jewel and Hylda Baker as brother and sister Eli and Nellie Pledge, who are forced to run a family pickle factory together in Lancashire. S6, E4: Workers' Playtime
A trip to the heart of Cumbria with a journey from Whitehaven to Carlisle, with a stunning coastline overlooking the Irish Sea and calling in at several small Cumbrian towns and villages on the way.

After being left in an industrial freezer, Terry looks for alternative employment and gets work helping out an old friend in the second hand furniture business. S5 E5: Second Hand Pose
Rose is imprisoned while trying to stop Elizabeth breaking the law. S2 E10: A Special Mischief
When Linda McCartney visits Liverpool to open an animal rescue centre, Billy sees his chance to sell sandwiches to the crowds outside. S5 E7
Harold has nowhere to take his lady friends and resolves to get himself a bachelor pad, but his love life doesn't go according to plan; meanwhile, Albert has a new lease of life. S4 E5: A Box in Town
Harry the signalman has fattened up a lovely turkey in time for Christmas, but the turkey seems to have other plans. S1 E2: Talking Turkey
Rigsby is looking for another lodger - one of his current lodgers, Miss Jones, is keen for her acquaintance Philip to move in because she is attracted to him. S1 E1: The New Lodger
George needs some money and so sells Mildred's sun lamp to Larry; later, when Mildred is upstairs, she sees her lamp and thinks that Robin has stolen it. S6 E4: The Sunshine Boys
Having missed the interview which Yvonne set up for him, Gary has to pretend to be ill. Back in 1942, Phoebe really is ill, but will 1990's medicine work in the past? S3 E9: Someone to Watch Over Me
The strike ends with no concessions won by the miners, Jessie's headmaster proposes to her, and Matt tells Jack that Dolly is pregnant and agrees to marry her. S1 E6: Empire Day on the Slag Heap
After being left in an industrial freezer, Terry looks for alternative employment and gets work helping out an old friend in the second hand furniture business. S5 E5: Second Hand Pose
John Sullivan's bittersweet 80s sitcom. Mr and Mrs Warrender plan a surprise for Penny's birthday, but Penny and Vince have a surprise of their own. S1 E6: Happy Birthday Penny
Jo sees a mouse in her bedroom and refuses to sleep in there; much to Chrissy's dismay, Jo swaps with Robin while the mouse is on the loose. S3 E4: Of Mice And Women
Classic sitcom featuring Frank Spencer, a well-meaning one-man disaster area. Betty persuades Frank to take a short course in public relations. S2 E3: The Public Relations Course
Sharon makes plans to take Tracey and Dorien on a madcap '70s weekender, but there is no dressing up soe news that Tracey is about to receive. S11 E7: Without a Trace
A selection of live performances from TV music shows in the 1960s for a genuine taste of the best of the '60s!
Harold has nowhere to take his lady friends and resolves to get himself a bachelor pad, but his love life doesn't go according to plan; meanwhile, Albert has a new lease of life. S4 E5: A Box in Town
Rigsby finally pops the question to Miss Jones with his decree absolute at long last in hand, but will she accept? S4 E6: Come On In The Water's Lovely

Terry is horrified when Bob refuses to go on a stag night and insists on a quiet night in, but pre-wedding nerves mean that Bob's plan to celebrate his nuptials sober goes awry. S1 E12: Boys' Night In
Arthur has a few difficult questions to answer concerning his choice of partner at the Winchester's quiz night. S9 E1: I'll Never Forget Whatsername
Joyce gets a public proposal from dodgy estate agent Monty, who, due to a mix up with identity cards, believes she is very wealthy. S8 E4
Ian returns from his travels, unaware that the car he's driving back to England contains a secret cargo. He makes an even more shocking discovery when he arrives back at the house.
When Mildred is away visiting her sister, George attempts to evict the tenants so that he and Jerry can convert the flat into separate units to increase rental income. S3 E6: We Shall Not Be Moved
A rude surprise lies in store when Rigsby is a little over-enthusiastic with advice after a couple of newlyweds move into the house. S4 E1: Hello Young Lovers
Joyce gets a public proposal from dodgy estate agent Monty, who, due to a mix up with identity cards, believes she is very wealthy. S8 E4
Danny Varrow is a great footballer but morally corrupt; he's a womaniser and a gambler, so Terry has his work cut out when his past catches up with him. S2 E11: All About Scoring, Innit
1985 Christmas special. Alf schemes so they can attend two Christmas dinners. Rita is forced to stay.
Chrissy receives flowers and chocolates from a secret admirer; it turns out that they were meant for Jo and that the admirer had wrongly assumed her to be Chrissy. S3 E5: Somebody Out there Likes Me
David and Linda use the situation to their advantage when Amy and Robert can't stand the accident-prone Neville. S3 E4: The Go-Between

Llangollen Railway was started in 1975 by a group of enthusiasts who saw the potential for a scenic heritage line through the Dee Valley from Llangollen for ten miles to the town of Corwen.

Justin James concocts a scheme with a former footballer to obtain tickets for an England v Scotland football match. S5 E6: The Long Ride Back to Scratchwood
Elizabeth has a new lover - wealthy businessman Julius Karekin - and the Bellamys could be about to lose Eaton Place. S2 E11: The Fruits of Love
Adrian's poems have been published and Joey receives a totally unexpected letter. Shifty tries to park a car in someone's living room. S5 E8
Harold has some big political ambitions and holds a local Labour Party meeting at home; Albert, a fervent Tory, sets out to disrupt it. S4 E6: My Old Man's a Tory
New station master Cecil Parkin is intent on making an impression at Hatley, but Jack is sure that he's already made an impression on his wife May. S1 E3: The Train Now Standing
Rigsby becomes convinced that magic is real after Philip bangs a ritual spear on the floor - while Ruth tries to offer herself to Rigsby, Philip and Alan play a trick on him. S1 E2: Black Magic
Robin's brother Norman comes to stay for two days and takes a shine to Chrissy; when the three of them go out to dinner, Robin gets food poisoning. S6 E5: Mum Always Liked You Best
Gary manages to overcome his jealousy enough to get Jeep parts for Ron, but his shopping trip is interrupted by an outbreak of racial tension. S3 E10: The Yanks Are Coming
Bella turns the front of the house into a shop using Bill's compensation, Jessie gets engaged to Arthur, and Jack marries Dolly at a registry office. S1 E7: A First Time for Everything
Justin James concocts a scheme with a former footballer to obtain tickets for an England v Scotland football match. S5 E6: The Long Ride Back to Scratchwood
John Sullivan's bittersweet sitcom. Penny is slightly apprehensive about her decision to set up home with Vince again. S1 E7: Moving In and Out Again
Chrissy receives flowers and chocolates from a secret admirer; it turns out that they were meant for Jo and that the admirer had wrongly assumed her to be Chrissy. S3 E5: Somebody Out there Likes Me
Classic sitcom. Betty has to break the news to Frank that she is pregnant, and Frank goes for a job at a holiday camp with predictably disastrous results. S2 E4: Frank and Marvin
Tracey and Sharon take a trip to a spa to cope with Tracey's shattering news and try to make plans for the future, and Dorien prepares to say a long goodbye to her daughter. S11 E8: Spa Wars
A selection of live performances from TV music shows in the 1960s for a genuine taste of the best of the '60s!
Harold has some big political ambitions and holds a local Labour Party meeting at home; Albert, a fervent Tory, sets out to disrupt it. S4 E6: My Old Man's a Tory
Rigsby is looking for another lodger - one of his current lodgers, Miss Jones, is keen for her acquaintance Philip to move in because she is attracted to him. S1 E1: The New Lodger
The day of the wedding dawns - the women are racing around getting ready for the big event while Terry has his hands full looking after a nervous Bob. S1 E13: End of an Era
Arthur acquires a hot air balloon in a bid to break into the aerial advertising game, but his latest venture is hijacked. S9 E2: No Way to Treat a Daley
Tiger and Joey attempt to climb a mountain, the Maltbys decide to cut their holiday short, and romance is in the air as both Eddie and Rob bag themselves a date each. S8 E5
Paddy is back. Desperate to conceal the truth of his whereabouts, Paddy spins Mimi a lie, but he has vengeance on his mind. S6 E8
Chrissy takes Robin's place at a weekly card game while he is in hospital having his tonsils removed - and claims that a woman can play cards just as well as a man. S3 E7: Three of a Kind
There's been a spare bed in Philip's room since Alan moved out, and Rigsby has no intentions of keeping it empty. S4 E2: Fire And Brimstone
